mission.title: contour: document how to run multiple Contours per cluster (e.g. internal and external)
mission.description: document how to run multiple Contours per cluster (e.g. internal and external). Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current contour deployment
steps.description:
Verify your contour version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
helm list -n contour 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working contour installation.

steps.title: Review contour configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant contour configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get configmap -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=contour
```
**Objective**

I need to configure internal and external ingress controllers on AWS with NLB 

Internal Ingress is served by an internal NLB (only accessible from within the VPC)
External Ingress is served by an external NLB (publicly

steps.title: Apply the fix for document how to run multiple Contours per cluster (e.g.…
steps.description:
Move the example deployment to Kustomize. This requires the `kustomize`
tool, since the version of Kustomize vendored in `kubectl apply -k`
is too old to support.

The YAML documents in the example deployment are broken into 4 components
located in `config/components` - types, contour, envoy and
```yaml
- serve
        - --incluster
        - --xds-address
        - 0.0.0.0
        - --xds-port
        - $(CONTOUR_EXTERNAL_SERVICE_PORT)
        - --envoy-http-port
        - "80"   #<<<<<
        - --envoy-https-port
        - "443" #<<<<<
        - --ingress-class-name=envoy-external
```

steps.title: Upgrade contour to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade contour:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade contour contour/contour --namespace contour
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n contour
helm list -n contour
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get events -n contour --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "document how to run multiple Contours per cluster (e.g.…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Move the example deployment to Kustomize. This requires the `kustomize`
tool, since the version of Kustomize vendored in `kubectl apply -k`
is too old to support.

The YAML documents in the example deployment are broken into 4 components
located in `config/components` - types, contour, envoy and certgen.
codeSnippets:
- name: NODE_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: spec.nodeName

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with contour installed or the issue environment reproducible.
